perturbation.original_text: TPC shall pay any Tournament Bonuses due Consolidated Artists for a Major Tournament win within sixty (60) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage and any Tournament Bonuses due Consolidated Artists for a network televised US PGA Tour Tournament win shall be paid within forty-five (45) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage. All other Tournament Bonuses due to Consolidated Artists shall be paid by TPC within thirty (30) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage.
perturbation.changed_text: TPC shall pay any Tournament Bonuses due Consolidated Artists for a Major Tournament win within ninety (90) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage and any Tournament Bonuses due Consolidated Artists for a network televised US PGA Tour Tournament win shall be paid within fifteen (15) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage. All other Tournament Bonuses due to Consolidated Artists shall be paid by TPC within forty-five (45) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment deadlines for Tournament Bonuses. Initially, Major Tournament wins had a 60-day payment window, network televised US PGA Tour Tournament wins had a 45-day window, and all other bonuses had a 30-day window. Now, Major Tournament wins have a 90-day payment window, network televised US PGA Tour Tournament wins have a 15-day window and all other bonuses have a 45-day window. These conflicting deadlines create uncertainty and potential disputes over when payments are actually due, particularly when considering the 'Time of the Essence' clause in Section 16.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TPC acknowledges that time is of the essence in the payment of all compensation due Consolidated Artists hereunder. For the purposes of this Agreement, all payments not received within thirty (30) days of the date due shall be deemed "past due". Such past due payments shall bear interest at a rate of two percent (2%) per month OR the maximum rate permissible by law, whichever is less. The imposition of interest provided for in this Paragraph shall be in addition to any other remedies available to Consolidated Artists under this Agreement or otherwise. Accordingly, Consolidated Artists shall not be precluded from exercising any other remedies, whether at law or in equity, to enforce the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment deadlines for Tournament Bonuses, specifying different timeframes (90 days, 15 days, and 45 days) for different types of bonuses. This directly contradicts the 'Time of the Essence' clause in Section 16, which states that payments not received within 30 days of the date due are considered 'past due'. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding when payments are actually due and when interest begins to accrue. The original text had differing values to, but the changed version now includes a value that goes against the contradicted text, which makes the existence of contradiction a definite yes.

perturbation.original_text: Simultaneous with the delivery of each statement setting forth such totals, TPC - 10 - agrees to pay Consolidated Artists the appropriate Royalty Compensation in excess of the $5,000 quarterly payment set forth in Paragraph 10 above, due on the sales of the Endorsed Product for the period covered by such statement.
perturbation.changed_text: Simultaneous with the delivery of each statement setting forth such totals, TPC - 10 - agrees to pay Consolidated Artists the appropriate Royalty Compensation in excess of the $5,000 quarterly payment set forth in Paragraph 11 above, due on the sales of the Endorsed Product for the period covered by such statement.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation involves changing the paragraph reference from 'Paragraph 10 above' to 'Paragraph 11 above' within Section 12. Paragraph 10 discusses Money List Bonuses, while Paragraph 11 discusses Royalty Compensation for Endorsed Products. By referencing Paragraph 11 (Royalty Compensation) in the royalty payment clause (Section 12), the text now refers to itself, potentially causing confusion over the calculation and payment of excess royalties, and contradicting with the general intent of the section which is to describe the statement delivary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Within thirty (30) days of the conclusion of each three (3) month period in each Contract Year during the Contract Period, TPC agrees to deliver to Consolidated Artists and Advantage an itemized statement setting forth the actual number of all Endorsed Products distributed and sold during the preceding three (3) month period. Simultaneous with the delivery of each statement setting forth such totals, TPC - 10 - agrees to pay Consolidated Artists the appropriate Royalty Compensation in excess of the $5,000 quarterly payment set forth in Paragraph 11 above, due on the sales of the Endorsed Product for the period covered by such statement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters a paragraph reference within a payment clause. Originally referring to Paragraph 10 (Money List Bonuses) and then changed to Paragraph 11 (Royalty Compensation), the payment clause (Section 12) now refers to itself. This creates confusion and a potential circular reference regarding the calculation and payment of excess royalties. The change creates an inconsistency, as per the definition provided in the Perturbation Definitions: "Inconsistencies also lead to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties." The contradicted text is the modified section which states "Simultaneous with the delivery of each statement setting forth such totals, TPC - 10 - agrees to pay Consolidated Artists the appropriate Royalty Compensation in excess of the $5,000 quarterly payment set forth in Paragraph 11 above, due on the sales of the Endorsed Product for the period covered by such statement." The original text referred to the correct section for determining the base royalty payment, whereas the changed text causes it to reference itself which contradicts the general intent to deliver a statement, and calculate royalties based on the number Paragraph 11 defines. Because the Royalty compensation defines itself, this creates an Inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: TPC shall have the right to prorate the Retainer Fee due Consolidated Artists upon fourteen (14) days written notice to Consolidated Artists or Advantage in the event Ogle fails to play a minimum of seventeen (17) U.S. PGA - 11 - Tour Events and the British Open in each Contract Year during the Contract Period.
perturbation.changed_text: TPC shall have the right to prorate the Retainer Fee due Consolidated Artists upon thirty (30) days written notice to Consolidated Artists or Advantage in the event Ogle fails to play a minimum of seventeen (17) U.S. PGA - 11 - Tour Events and the British Open in each Contract Year during the Contract Period. However, if Ogle plays in at least 10 U.S. PGA Tour Events but fewer than 17, the Retainer Fee will not be prorated.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction by altering the conditions under which TPC can prorate the Retainer Fee. The initial agreement states that TPC can prorate the fee if Ogle fails to play a minimum of 17 U.S. PGA Tour Events and the British Open, with a fourteen-day notice. The change introduces an additional condition: If Ogle plays in at least 10 U.S. PGA Tour Events but fewer than 17, the Retainer Fee will not be prorated, along with a thirty-day notice requirement. Now, there's an inconsistency because it's unclear whether TPC can prorate the fee if Ogle plays, say, 15 events. This change affects the overall 'payment' obligation, specifically the conditions affecting the Retainer Fee payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consolidated Artists shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to TPC in the event of the occurrence of any of the following contingencies: (a) If TPC is adjudicated as insolvent, declares bankruptcy or fails to continue its business of selling the Endorsed Product; or (b) If TPC fails to make payment to Consolidated Artists of any sums due pursuant to this Agreement within thirty (30) days following the date such payment is due hereunder, provided that TPC is notified in writing of such nonpayment by Consolidated Artists or Advantage and such payment is not made within ten (10) days following such notification. TPC agrees that such termination shall not relieve it of its obligation to pay Consolidated Artists all compensation contemplated hereunder. Accordingly, Consolidated Artists shall not waive any of its rights at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition where the retainer fee will not be prorated if Ogle plays in at least 10 U.S. PGA Tour Events but fewer than 17. This contradicts the contradicted text because if TPC fails to prorate the retainer fee due to this new condition, Consolidated Artists could claim that TPC has failed to make payment of sums due pursuant to this agreement, potentially leading to contract termination. This directly impacts the 'payment' obligation and introduces inconsistency regarding when and how payments can be adjusted, conflicting with the termination clause triggered by non-payment.
